title: Evangelion Shito Ikusei
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Shito Ikusei?             Quote Post by Nytloc » Fri Mar 30, 2012 3:23 am I've recently come across a site called Vizzed that lets you play old video games... long story short, I started playing the N64 Evangelion game and was having fun with it when I realized that there was a second Eva game I'd never heard about. I know absolutely nothing about the Japanese language, but the scenario seems interesting and bizarre at the same time. Apparently, it takes place just as Kaji is handing the Adam Embryo off to Gendo, and you play as Kaji doing random stuff around NERV HQ. Ritsuko talked to me, I was supposed to choose Maya, Aoba, or Hyuga. I chose Maya because I don't yet have a firm grasp of the controls. By far the most interesting thing is... Apparently the Adam embryo is released from its block, and I'm supposed to raise it! Like a tomogatchi or something. As I was typing this, I apparently waited to long and it died. Or something. It just shriveled up, and I hit some buttons, and it sprung back up. Really bizarre. I have no idea what I'm doing, but is there any more information on this game? I sincerely doubt this should be taken seriously, but I know that some Eva games have interesting commentaries from the creators. I can just imagine Kaji running around Nerv HQ training baby Adam for its fated day of fusing with Gendo's hand. XP http://www.vizzed.com/play/neon-genesis-evangelion-shito-ikusei-ws-online-wonderswan-36957-game A link. Google comes up with very little on this game, except to confirm that it is a "raising" game. Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Sun Apr 01, 2012 3:22 pm "Angel Raising" was released for the Bandai WonderSwan in 1999, which was during the era of the Tamagotchi craze so it's little surprise that something Eva-themed would come up along these lines.
